mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit growth expands while industrial production stagnates, it indicates demand is being fueled by debt rather than real output, leading to earnings disappointments and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-credit/low-production regimes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator of near-term economic resilience, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as investors price in a soft landing.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rises while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects expanding corporate earnings capacity, which mechanically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations adjust to higher fundamental growth.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in total nonfarm payrolls signals an imminent earnings recession, causing institutional deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the payroll contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces consumer credit expansion, it indicates supply-side efficiency gains are driving growth without excessive leverage, supporting positive forward returns as margin expansion accelerates.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-production/low-credit regimes are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, a stable federal funds rate signals a policy error that will overheat the economy, prompting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets price in imminent rate cuts.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where CPI accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains flat or declines.
